design_philosophy.core_loop: Race through championship series, earn credits to upgrade vehicle components (engine, gearbox, tyres, armour, boost), compete against increasingly faster AI opponents across multiple difficulty tiers
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (fuel and vehicle integrity) combined with competitive racing; players must balance speed with recharge/repair pit stops, while AI opponents grow stronger without visible upgrades
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Futuristic outlawed racer competing in a galaxy-wide millennium championship for 'riches beyond belief' in a dystopian world where fun has been systematically suppressed
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy emphasis on vehicle customization as the primary progression mechanic; weapons are purely self-beneficial rather than competitive (no PvP weapon effects), forcing players to rely on skill and optimization rather than offensive gameplay; fuel/damage management creates environmental puzzle-solving within racing context

difficulty.checkpointing: Race-by-race progression with credit accumulation persisting across championship
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three difficulty settings that extend championship length and increase AI speed; easy and medium restrict highest-tier component availability, preventing full optimization on lower difficulties

progression.upgrade_system: Six customizable vehicle components unlocked progressively: engine, gearbox, tyres, armour, boost level (1-6 tiers); nuclear fusion engines and exotic materials (cobalt-titanium, liquid polymer) unlock at higher progression; weapons system (jump, warp, attractor) unlocks separately
progression.unlock_mechanism: Championship progression automatically gates component availability; AI opponents become faster without purchasing upgrades, creating escalating difficulty pressure
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty settings with different championship lengths and AI speeds
Secret bonuses (five total, though one incompletely implemented) awarded for specific driver activities
Split-track technology allows same race to have variable routing with intentionally different speeds
Customizable button layout per player session
Two-player simultaneous and single-player vs AI modes

world_building_techniques: Instruction manual exposition: detailed dystopian backstory (World War XVII 500 years prior, Galactic Conglomerate's thought suppression regime)
Title sequence introduction: galaxy-wide racing campaign premise every millennium
Environmental naming: futuristic track design, planet variety across series
Character motivation: outlaw thrill-seekers with excess wealth circumventing systematic fun-suppression

faction_indicators: Outlaw racing subculture vs. Galactic Conglomerate authority
Wealthy thrill-seekers willing to risk 'all' for the competition
danger_signals: World War XVII left colonized planets devastated
Systematic suppression of 'radical thought or action' by Bureau of Reasonable Entertainment
Anything 'remotely resembling fun has been analyzed and sterilized'
environmental_storytelling: Futuristic technology (nuclear fusion engines, warp devices, magnetic attractors)
Multi-planet racing across twelve star systems
Recharge and repair strips as infrastructure within tracks
AI opponent roster with named competitors across championship
